stimulus: In a 2021 review of 320 case histories, clinicians observed a broad pattern of stimulus-driven plasticity in which musicians showed faster cross-modal recruitment—______blank the recruitment of brain regions ordinarily devoted to one sense in the processing of input from another. String players (e.g., violinists) showed the largest effect; vocalists (e.g., choristers), meanwhile, showed the smallest.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. "That is" logically signals that the information that follows in the sentence clarifies the term "cross-modal recruitment" used in the previous discussion of the clinicians' findings.

Choice A is incorrect because "in addition" illogically signals that the information that follows merely adds a new fact to the findings, when in fact it defines a term introduced earlier in the sentence. Choice C is incorrect because "likewise" illogically signals that the information that follows is parallel to the findings, when it actually clarifies a term used in the description of those findings. Choice D is incorrect because "for example" illogically signals that the information provides a specific example of the findings, when it instead defines the technical term introduced earlier in the sentence.
